sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 88:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 88:1
items.html: (See above item 82.) Disparity of form separates and distinguishes spiritual things. Know that this oppositeness of form from one end to the other separates and drives them to opposite ends until there is no suckling between them whatsoever. You already know that there is only a will to bestow in the upper light, and not to receive even a bit. The shells are of completely opposite form, meaning wanting only to receive and swallow everything; they have no will to bestow whatsoever. For that reason they are separated from the Life of Lives and are therefore called “dead”, and so is anything that cleaves to them. (Part 3, Chapter 10, item 2)
